prompt: Tell me a joke that you actually find funny, and briefly say why you like it.
response:
Why don't scientists trust atoms?

Because they make up everything.

I find this joke funny because it plays on a double meaning of the word "make up." Atoms are the building blocks of matter, so they literally "make up" everything around us. However, the phrase "to make up" can also mean to fabricate or lie, which is why scientists, who value honesty and accuracy in their work, would distrust something that is not true or reliable. The unexpected shift in meaning from the literal to the figurative sense of the phrase "make up" is what makes this joke amusing to me.
